page: Amazon Web Services c5a.16xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor based on the x86_64 architecture, delivering 64 vCPUs across 32 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz and includes L1d, L2, and L3 caches of 32 KB, 0.5 MB, and 16 MB respectively. The instance is configured with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware, relying on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance profile. Single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with stress-ng single-core CPU tests placing in the bottom 10%. However, multi-core capabilities are more robust, with stress-ng multi-core CPU, Geekbench multi-core, and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression all registering in the middle 50%. The instance demonstrates strong capabilities in specific areas, scoring in the top 25% for Passmark CPU, extended instructions, encryption, and database operations. Redis SET operations also perform strongly, reaching 58.2 million operations per second. For machine learning workloads, LLM inference speeds for small and medium models remain average, though text generation for large 70B models drops to the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c5a.16xlarge offers a high-density compute profile using AMD EPYC processors, which typically provide a favorable performance-to-cost ratio compared to alternative architectures for multi-threaded workloads. The lack of bundled local storage and GPUs makes it a specialized option for compute-heavy tasks that do not require local NVMe drives. This configuration is suited for multi-threaded batch processing, software compilation, database operations, and distributed web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services c5a.16xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with 64 vCPUs, 32 physical cores, and 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark results indicate a tradeoff between poor single-core performance (bottom 10% in stress-ng) and strong multi-core and specialized capabilities, with top 25% rankings in Passmark CPU, encryption, and database operations. Redis SET operations are also strong at 58.2 million operations per second. This resource density of 2.0 GB RAM per vCPU makes it a cost-efficient option for multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, database operations, and web serving, while it remains less suited for single-threaded tasks or large-scale LLM text generation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5a.16xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and 20 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core performance for database and compute workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5a.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor.
tagline: An x86_64 compute-optimized configuration featuring 64 vCPUs and 128 GB RAM, delivering strong multi-threaded performance for database and compilation workloads.

128.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 2.0 GB per vCPU
Strong performance in Passmark CPU, encryption, and database operations
